title: India's refiners face headwinds after months of windfall profits
section_path: India's refiners face headwinds after months of windfall profits
section_path_text: India's refiners face headwinds after months of windfall profits
summary:
Main
By Serena Huang
Ιndia's refiners have been enjoying windfall profits from sky-high domestic and export margins in recent months, further sweetened by access to discounted Russian crude. In the recent week however, the global oil market seems to be tethering on the edge of a turning point, with clean product cracks retreating consecutively for days, pointing to an unwinding of the product market tightness. Are the winds of fortune turning for India's refiners?

children.title: Main
children.summary:
By Serena Huang
Ιndia's refiners have been enjoying windfall profits from sky-high domestic and export margins in recent months, further sweetened by access to discounted Russian crude. In the recent week however, the global oil market seems to be tethering on the edge of a turning point, with clean product cracks retreating consecutively for days, pointing to an unwinding of the product market tightness.

children.title: Indian refiners raise runs near operating limits in June, but signs of slowdown emerging
section_path: Indian refiners raise runs near operating limits in June, but signs of slowdown emerging
children.section_path_text: Indian refiners raise runs near operating limits in June, but signs of slowdown emerging
children.summary: Our assessment of Indian refiners' implied refinery runs, calculated using crude imports net stock change, indicate that refiners have revved up crude throughputs to near operating limits last month. A seasonal uptick in domestic harvesting and road freight activities, coupled with a demand pull from the tight global clean products market have incentivised refiners to push runs to multi-year highs.

children.title: Diverging trends of Russia Baltic/ Black Sea versus Far East crude loadings to India
section_path: Diverging trends of Russia Baltic/ Black Sea versus Far East crude loadings to India
children.section_path_text: Diverging trends of Russia Baltic/ Black Sea versus Far East crude loadings to India
children.summary: Imports of discounted Russian crude have surged to over 1.1mbd in June, marking a historical high. But recent weeks have seen a slowdown in Russian Baltic and Black Sea crude cargoes heading to India, with loadings in June sliding to a three-month low of 450kbd.

children.title: Clean product export hurdles ahead
children.summary: With export margins surpassing domestic profitability, some refiners have sought to increase exports to maximise profits while leaving their pumps dry in the domestic market. To ensure energy security, the Indian government has imposed regulations requiring exporters to sell a volume equivalent of 30% and 50% of their diesel and gasoline exports to the domestic market respectively for the fiscal year ending next March, while also implementing export taxes on the fuels to reign in exports.

children.title: India a crucial diesel supplier to Europe next year
section_path: India a crucial diesel supplier to Europe next year
children.section_path_text: India a crucial diesel supplier to Europe next year
children.summary: India is the world's third largest diesel exporter with 630kbd leaving the country so far this year, ranking behind the US (1mbd) and Russia (950kbd), but still slightly ahead of Saudi Arabia (610kbd). As such, the country is of crucial importance once Europe fully phases out diesel imports from Russia early next year.
